questionText: The weights, $X$ grams, of bags of sugar are modelled by $X \sim N(500, 10^2)$. Using the $68$–$95$–$99.7$ rule, estimate the percentage of bags weighing between $480$ g and $520$ g.

steps.explanation: The weights are normal with mean $\mu = 500$ g and standard deviation $\sigma = 10$ g.

steps.description: Compare the limits with the mean and sd
steps.explanation: Each limit is two standard deviations from the mean, so the interval is $\mu \pm 2\sigma$.

steps.description: Recall the relevant part of the rule
steps.explanation: The $68$–$95$–$99.7$ rule says about $95\%$ of a normal distribution lies within two standard deviations of the mean.

steps.description: Apply the rule to this interval
steps.explanation: Since $480$ and $520$ are exactly $\mu \pm 2\sigma$, the required probability is the $95\%$ figure.
So about $95\%$ of the bags weigh between $480$ g and $520$ g.
This is the estimated percentage, read straight from the empirical rule.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: About $95\%$ of the bags weigh between $480$ g and $520$ g.
